stories.title: Trump administration asks high court to OK its unprecedented immigration detention policy
stories.summary: The Trump administration is  asking the Supreme Court  to bless its massive expansion of ICE detention — a policy that federal district courts have  overwhelmingly rejected . Solicitor General John Sauer is urging the justices to resolve whether people residing in the U.S. without incident for years — sometimes decades — may be subject to mandatory ICE detention while their deportation proceedings play out. It’s a fight that could determine the fate of millions of people, and it c

stories.title: Trump administration partially lifts Anthropic’s AI export ban
stories.summary: The Trump administration on Friday partially rescinded its export ban on Anthropic’s most advanced artificial intelligence model — deescalating a confrontation that has caused confusion across the American AI industry . The release clears the way for a select group of more than 100 companies and agencies to gain access to the Mythos 5 model, two weeks after the administration imposed restrictions amid fears that the software could be used to launch cyberattacks. But a second advanced Anthropic m

stories.title: ‘Many are still afraid’: hope and caution in Budapest before first Pride since Orbán
stories.summary: LGBTQ+ people continue to reel from stigma spread by 16 years of rightwing populist government, says organiser One year ago they marched in record numbers, risking fines and facial recognition technology to challenge Viktor Orbán and his government’s escalating crackdown on LGBTQ+ rights. On Saturday, Hungarians will again take to the streets for Budapest Pride, this time in a march marked by the country’s sweeping political changes. The event, which is expected to unfold peacefully after police

stories.title: Erdoğan launches crackdown ahead of NATO summit
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Turkish authorities have carried out hundreds of arrests, banned protests and curbed media freedoms ahead of the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara, with campaigners accusing the government of using the gathering as a pretext for a clampdown on civil society. On Tuesday, Turkish police detained 225 people including academics, teachers and activists on suspicion of links to militant groups like the Islamic State — among them a 79-year-old environmental campaigner . The city of Ankara has a

stories.title: All quiet on the eastern flank? Nato leaders fear they can no longer rely on US help if Russia attacks
stories.summary: Trump administration’s rhetoric has created so much uncertainty that Poland and Baltic states have fresh doubts as alliance prepares to meet next month A nightmare scenario has been playing on eastern European minds with increasing intensity since Donald Trump returned to the White House: what if Russia attacks and the US does not join the fight? On the rare occasions the question is posed out loud, nobody much likes the answer. In mid-May, at a gathering in Tallinn, the US undersecretary of sta

stories.title: Germany braced for temperatures ‘well over 40C’ as Europe heatwave pushes east – live
stories.summary: Forecasters say hottest conditions are now spreading into central and eastern Europe, including Poland and the Balkans The UK is set to get some respite from this week’s record-breaking heat over the weekend, but much of the rest of Europe remains in the grip of an intense heatwave as it shifts eastwards. Germany provisionally recorded its highest ever temperature yesterday with 41.3C near the city of Saarbrücken close to the French border. Forecasters say the hottest conditions are now spreadin

stories.title: 'Operation Purgatory': Magyar moves to demolish Orban system
stories.summary: The new Hungarian government is moving swiftly to dismantle the system of former PM Viktor Orban. The focus is on fighting corruption, reforming the media and protecting democracy.

stories.title: UK June heat record broken again, France postpones Pride and Poland warns of wildfires as heatwave grips Europe – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here: UK June heat record broken for third day in a row Fourth child dies in France as brutal heatwave forecast to shift east Over in the UK, firefighters are still trying to bring a large wildfire in Derbyshire under control. The blaze, which has burned over 500 square metres of moorland and woodland on Tintwistle Moor, near Glossop, broke out on Wednesday evening, with fire crews from Manchester and Derbyshire deploying a water-dropping

stories.title: Szenegál tönkreverte a szinte végig emberhátrányban játszó Irakot, és még van esélye továbbjutni
stories.summary: Hamar vezetést szereztek, aztán hamar kerültek emberelőnybe is, a második félidőben pedig feljebb tudtak kapcsolni, és lőttek még négyet a fáradó irakiaknak. A vége 5-0 lett.

stories.title: Bod Péter Ákos: Nincs királyi út, új állammodell kell
stories.summary: Még túl sem jutott a kormány a legsürgetőbb korrekciók megtételén, hamarosan be kell mutatnia adópolitikáját, vagyongazdálkodási elveit, végső soron azt, hogy milyennek képzeli el a magyar államot.

stories.title: Hőkupola: ettől forr fel Magyarország a következő napokban
stories.summary: Miután egész Európán átvonul a rekordmelegeket hozó időjárás, a jövő hét elejére itthon is közel kerülhetünk a mindenkori maximumhoz. Már szombattól harmadfokú hőségriasztás van érvényben. Meteorológust kérdeztünk, mit kell tudni a hőkupola jelenségéről, amiről egyre többet hallani mostanában.

stories.title: Belgium elkezdett játszani, Új-Zélandot kiütve csoportgyőztesen jutottak tovább a vb-n
stories.summary: Trossard és De Bruyne felpörgette a belgákat, a vége 5-1 lett.

stories.title: Eltűnnek a falusi kocsmák, az idő a kistelepüléseken is ellenük dolgozik
stories.summary: Évről évre kevesebb a kocsma Magyarországon, ahol ez egykor a kistelepülések lakóinak találkozóhelye volt. A vendégek is a tulajdonosok generációját nem követik újak. Van még jövőjük a kocsmáknak? A Deutsche Welle videója.

stories.title: A globalizáció sokkja semmi lesz ahhoz képest, ha az óriásvállalatokra hagyjuk, mit kezdenek az új technológiákkal
stories.summary: Dani Rodrik közgazdász, a Harvard professzora szerint Magyarország vezetésének tudatosítania kell, hogy már nem lehet az iparra építeni a gazdaság jövőjét. Az még nem dőlt el, hogy a globalizáció után milyen új világban fogunk élni, de a legsötétebb disztópia elkerüléséhez a kormányoknak a sarkukra kell állniuk.

stories.title: Az Óriás-hegység, ahol Hitler kastélybunkere versenyzik a turistákért egy természetes sziklalabirintussal
stories.summary: Egy történelmi reneszánsz-barokk kastély alatt húzódó náci betonbunker-rendszer és egy természetes sziklalabirintus vetekszik egymással Délnyugat-Lengyelországban az Óriás-hegység lábánál, ahol az Alsó-sziléziai vajdaság meghívására jártunk a napokban. Ha már ott voltunk, megmásztuk a hegység 1603 méter magas csúcsát, a Śnieżkát is.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave expected to bring Germany and Poland to 40C
stories.summary: Austrian Grand Prix declares heat hazard as dramatic rise in temperatures forecast in central and northern Europe Temperatures are forecast to rise dramatically in parts of central and northern Europe this weekend as the intense heatwave continues . In Germany and Poland, highs up to or exceeding 40C (104F) are expected on both Saturday and Sunday, days after swathes of France experienced similar extreme temperatures. The Austrian Grand Prix, taking place this weekend in Spielberg, has declared

stories.title: Új főispánokat nevezett ki Magyar Péter hét vármegye élére
stories.summary: Középtávon megszűnnek majd a vármegyék, a kormányhivataloktól is több hatáskör elkerül.

stories.title: Az Orbán-rendszer a lelkek megrontására ment rá, de kudarcot vallottak
stories.summary: A Fidesz melegellenessége a fiatalok elvesztéséhez és a választási vereséghez is hozzájárulhatott. A melegházasság és az örökbefogadás támogatottsága történelmi csúcson: de bevállalja a Tisza a jogegyenlőséget?

stories.title: Szombaton megszűnik a közmédia jelenlegi vezetőinek megbízatása
stories.summary: Távozik Koltay András, a Médiatanács elnöke és a testület tagjai, Altorjai Anita, a Duna Médiaszolgáltató Nonprofit Zrt. vezetője és Papp Dániel, az MTVA vezérigazgatója.

stories.title: Trump gyors és olcsó megoldást ígért egy drága és bonyolult problémára. Meg is lett az eredménye
stories.summary: A Lincoln-emlékműnél lévő tükörmedence Washington egyik jelképe. A felújítás után a kékre festett medence előbb zöldre változott az elburjánzó algáktól, aztán elkezdett feljönni a frissen lerakott festék, a tisztítóberendezések meghibásodtak, és még egy döglött kacsát is fotóztak a vízben. Trumpnak nem kellett sok, hogy megtalálja a valódi felelősöket, és tényleg történt vandalizmus is a medencénél.

stories.title: Germany news: Major warship project to be scrapped
stories.summary: The Defense Ministry is dropping a multi-billion-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. And train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight halted Germany's rail network . DW has more.

stories.title: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens
stories.summary: Polish President Karol Nawrocki last week stripped Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor. How did it come to this? And what has it got to do with next year's election in Poland?

stories.title: To defend against hybrid attacks, governments should team up with the private sector
stories.summary: Elisabeth Braw is a senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, author of the award-winning “Goodbye Globalization” and a regular columnist for POLITICO. Her new book, “ The Undersea War ,” is out later this year. From cyberattacks to arson and drone surveillance, these days European companies are being targeted by all manner of nefarious attacks. These businesses are crucial to the daily functioning of our nations; indeed, that may be the reason they’re being targeted. And yet, in most countries, bu

US says it struck Iran targets after attack on cargo ship on the strait of Hormuz Sign up for the Breaking News US email Hayes went on: As national security adviser to the president of the United States, Mr Bolton had access to and was responsible for safeguarding the most sensitive national defense information, including classified material. Mr Bolton knew how to handle classified information, where it should be stored, how it should be stored, and with whom he cou

stories.title: Hikers lost in Kosciuszko national park rescued within five hours by AI drone
stories.summary: Fire and Rescue NSW uses thermal imaging and a mobile phone red light to quickly locate men who veered off walking track near Jindabyne Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Two hikers who veered off a walking track in Kosciuszko national park have been found within five hours using a drone powered by artificial intelligence, a first-of-its-kind mission, Fire and Rescue NSW (FRNSW) has said. The two men, aged in their 20s, were reported missing at 7pm on Tuesday evening af

stories.title: Iran war: US military hits Iran over cargo vessel attack
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has accused Iran of carrying out a drone attack on a cargo vessel on Thursday and violating the US-Iran deal. In response, the US military has launched fresh strikes on Iran. DW has more.

stories.title: US launches strikes against Iran following drone attack on cargo ship
stories.summary: Iranian state media reported that strikes hit a telecommunications mast in the Sirik area of southern Iran.

stories.title: ‘More people die in the winter’: US energy chief downplays Europe’s deadly heat wave
stories.summary: U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright had a message for Europe as it bakes beneath a record heat wave this week: Stop your whining. “Always more people die in the winter than die in the summer, because cold is a vastly larger killer than heat is,” he said this week in video remarks delivered to the Alliance for Responsible Citizenship conference, a gathering of influential conservative figures, many of whom dispute the facts of climate change. Wright zeroed in on European deaths in the winter of 20

stories.title: US strikes Iran after ship attack
stories.summary: The U.S. conducted a strike against Iran on Friday in response to an attack on a commercial ship passing through the Strait of Hormuz, the most significant test yet of the deal to end hostilities that President Donald Trump signed last week. U.S. Central Command said Friday that American aircraft struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites, along with coastal radar sites. On Thursday,  Iran attacked a Singaporean cargo ship  that was exiting the strait along the Omani coast with a o

stories.title: Why Europe’s heat waves are still so deadly
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Once the heat dissipates, Europe will start counting its dead.  The extraordinary temperatures sweeping the western part of the continent this week will have killed hundreds, likely thousands, more than two decades after the deadly summer of 2003 alerted governments to the dangers of extreme heat.  While a first estimate of the death toll will take weeks, Spanish researchers are already attributing more than 210 fatalities between Sunday and Wednesday to the heat.

stories.title: Trump threatens additional tariffs on European countries using digital service taxes
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday threatened a 100 percent tariff on European countries that impose digital service taxes on American corporations. In a  post to social media , the president said “numerous European countries“ are discussing or “close to actually doing this.” “Please let this statement serve to represent that any Country that imposes such a Tax will immediately be met with a 100% TARIFF on any and all Goods sent to the United States of America,” Trump said. “This TARIFF

stories.title: US says it struck Iran targets after attack on cargo ship in the strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Strikes against military facilities were in response to drone attack a day earlier on a cargo vessel The US has struck Iran in a tit-for-tat response to a drone strike on a cargo ship, as the ceasefire between the US and Iran that reopened the strait of Hormuz undergoes its greatest test yet. The US strikes targeted multiple missile and drone facilities in Iran near the strait of Hormuz and on Qeshm Island on Friday in what appeared to be a limited strike meant to respond to Iran’s attack on a S

stories.title: Trump attacks Democrats as ‘godless communists’ in test of midterms message
stories.summary: President seizes on wins by Mamdani-backed candidates to warn darkly of threat to ‘traditional American way of life’ Donald Trump has previewed a Republican strategy for the midterm elections, seizing on a progressive sweep in New York to portray Democrats as “godless communists” who pose an existential threat to the nation. The US president, who was a child during the “red scare”, seized on wins by democratic socialists backed by the mayor of New York City, Zohran Mamdani , to stoke fears that

stories.title: Gavin Newsom urges a national 'billionaires' tax' while fighting one in California
stories.summary: California governor calls for national tax on super-wealthy and suggests the US should own a stake in AI companies US politics live – latest updates California’s governor, Gavin Newsom , called for a national “billionaires tax” on Friday as he fights a ballot measure targeting the ultra-wealthy in his home state. Newsom, who is expected to run for president in 2028, published his proposal the day after California officials certified a ballot measure to levy a one-time 5% tax on residents worth m

stories.title: Polygamous sect leader guilty of abuse after girls found in trailer on highway
stories.summary: Self-proclaimed prophet Samuel Bateman already serving 50-year prison sentence over child sexual abuse A polygamous sect leader already serving a 50-year federal prison sentence for orchestrating sex involving children was convicted Friday on state child abuse charges after girls were found in an unventilated trailer he was hauling through Arizona . Someone alerted authorities about the trailer in 2022 after seeing small fingers reaching through gaps in the doors. Police stopped Samuel Bateman’s

stories.title: Australian man arrested in Thailand after 17-year-old’s body found in suitcase
stories.summary: The 46-year-old was stopped at about 9.30pm on Friday while preparing to board a Jetstar flight to Perth, according to police and local media Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An Australian man has been arrested at a Thai airport in connection with the alleged murder of a 17-year-old girl whose naked body was found in a suitcase, according to local police. The man , 46, was stopped at about 9.30pm on Friday while preparing to travel on a Jetstar flight to Perth, accord

stories.title: Trump blames Iran for drone strike on cargo ship in strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US president calls it ‘foolish violation’ of ceasefire agreement after drone damaged ship’s upper deck US politics live – latest updates Donald Trump on Friday blamed Iran for carrying out a drone strike on a cargo ship in the strait of Hormuz, calling it a “foolish violation” of the ceasefire agreement with the US. One drone damaged the upper deck of the ship but the ship was able to proceed, the US president said. The US shot down three other drones aimed at the ship, he said. Continue reading

stories.title: Montenegro arrests Iran-linked hacker wanted by US
stories.summary: The FBI was helping the Montenegrin police detain an Iranian-Turkish national whose cyberattacks on US infrastructure allegedly caused billions in damages.

stories.title: Small plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building
stories.summary: Police and emergency crews sealed off the area around Beijing's tallest skyscraper after a small plane crash damaged the building. Images from the scene showed a large hole in the upper floors of the 528-metre CITIC Tower, while witnesses reported seeing aircraft debris at the base of the building.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement after US-brokered talks
stories.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

stories.title: Searches continue for Venezuela earthquake survivors
stories.summary: More than 520 emergency responders from across the EU have been mobilised to support search and rescue efforts in Venezuela, the European Commission said.

stories.title: US sanctions Rwanda gold refinery accused of smuggling DR Congo's minerals
stories.summary: The US alleges Gasabo Gold Refinery smuggled at least 60kg of gold in early 2026.

stories.title: Britain’s next leader faces a big call on North Sea drilling. Donald Trump is watching.
stories.summary: LONDON — Labour MPs and climate campaigners are abandoning resistance to a controversial new North Sea gas field ahead of Andy Burnham’s expected entry into Downing Street next month — but they say they’ll carry on fighting over a second, even more contentious, oil field.   Burnham, who is on a path to becoming Labour leader and U.K. prime minister within weeks, is under pressure from the party’s union backers to approve Jackdaw, a major new gas development 150 miles east of Aberdeen i

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll reaches 920 as interim president vows to save ‘as many as possible’
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez says foreign rescue teams are arriving as anger grows at official response and limited resources Survivors tell of ‘brutal and fast’ Venezuela quake as hunt for survivors goes on Venezuela’s interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, has vowed to fight to save “as many people as possible” as the official death toll from the country’s worst earthquake in more than a century almost doubled, but frustration was growing at the perceived sluggishness of the government’s response. Rodríguez’s

stories.title: Utah under historic ‘red flag’ weather warning amid dangerous wildfires
stories.summary: Governor also announced state of emergency and banned personal fireworks in state until after Fourth of July Salt Lake City’s National Weather Service declared a “particularly dangerous situation red flag warning”, the first in the service’s history, according to a social media post. “THIS IS EXTREMELY RARE … Wind gusts of 40-50+ mph, combined with hot temperatures and extremely dry air, could cause explosive wildfire growth. Any new fire that starts could spread rapidly and become difficult to

stories.title: Snow and ice on Swiss glaciers melting at alarming rate amid heatwave, expert says
stories.summary: Accumulation on Switzerland’s glaciers from last winter expected to all be gone by Monday amid ‘enormous’ melt rates across Alps Swiss glaciers are set to lose an enormous amount of ice due to the heatwave battering Europe, according to the head of Glacier Monitoring in Switzerland (Glamos). The snow and ice accumulated last winter by Switzerland’s glaciers is expected to have all melted away by Monday, marking the alarming second-earliest arrival on record of the tipping point known as glacier

stories.title: Do you really need to speak German to take a cooling dip? This row in Halle raises all manner of red flags | Fatma Aydemir
stories.summary: A pool manager invoked safety to bar non-German speakers during the heatwave. With the far right soaring, the move is making everyone less safe Humans are vulnerable in water. Beaches have red flags; swimming pools have flashy warning signs to remind us of our vulnerability when we just want to cool down in the midst of a searing heatwave. Pool rules are essential, especially when children are around, or tourists who don’t know about the local safety measures. With pictograms and whistling lifeg

stories.summary: As a host of countries move to rein in social media use by children, could this be technology’s big tobacco moment? Continue reading...

stories.title: Venice protest planned for US ambassador’s superyacht visit
stories.summary: Activists aim to repeat disruption of Jeff Bezos’s wedding when billionaire Tilman Fertitta drops anchor Protesters in Venice are planning to disrupt a visit by the billionaire US ambassador to Italy in his 117-metre superyacht, which they fear he plans to dock in the lagoon city. “We ruined the party for Jeff Bezos’s wedding last year – this year let’s ruin the ambassador’s tour!” said Stella Faye, a 28-year-old researcher and activist, at a meeting of about 40 demonstrators on Thursday. Contin

stories.title: Parents booking air-conditioned hotels to keep babies safe in UK heatwave
stories.summary: Hotels report increase in last-minute bookings, including from families attempting to escape hot homes Families, including parents with newborn babies, are booking air-conditioned rooms in hotels to escape the UK heatwave, with companies reporting a surge in demand. Data from the accommodation reservation website Booking.com shows that since 1 June, the share of searches using the “air-conditioning” filter has tripled across Great Britain coinciding with the latest heatwave in northern Europe. C

stories.title: Where Copenhagen leads, the food world still follows
stories.summary: Two decades after chefs rewrote the rules at Noma, Copenhagen’s food scene still flies the flag for seasonality and innovation – progressive, sustainable and uniquely Danish I didn’t realise I was a fussy eater until I left Denmark. During 12 years of living Danishly, with regular trips to the capital, I just … liked most things. Danes specialise in high-quality, organic produce, eaten as close to its natural state as possible. Denmark has very specific, diverse climatic conditions, making seaso

stories.title: Screen time can damage under-twos’ development, landmark study suggests
stories.summary: Exclusive: Researchers call for urgent investigation of risks to babies of tablets, smartphones and other digital devices Screen time for babies and toddlers under the age of two has been linked with long-term negative effects on health and quality of life and should be avoided, according to a landmark study . It warns that using screens during that period may lead to wide-ranging developmental concerns and calls for further urgent investigation of the risks smartphones, tablets and other digita

stories.title: Today programme suffers ‘body blow’ as BBC prioritises social and digital content
stories.summary: Staff at Radio 4 show, which has 5 million listeners, told making content for likes of TikTok will take precedence for correspondents For decades, the task of briefing the nation on Radio 4’s agenda-setting Today programme has been one of the most urgent tasks facing the BBC’s top journalists. stories.title: Outrage as woman jailed for three years after criticising Somali government online
stories.summary: Sentencing of 27-year-old Sadia Moalim Ali condemned by former president and prime ministers as well as rights groups A rickshaw driver in Somalia has been sentenced to three years in prison for comments she made on social media, in a case that has caught the public’s attention and provoked outrage in the country. Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old nursing graduate, was originally charged with insulting government institutions and incitement to commit a crime, but convicted only of the former. Her

stories.title: Spain's military firefighters battle wildfire in Huesca province – video
stories.summary: Firefighters worked to halt a wildfire near Tamarite de Litera in Huesca province, where flames have scorched at least 1,000 hectares (2,470 acres). Firefighters from the military emergency unit worked through Thursday night to contain the blaze, clearing vegetation and using hoses to prevent the fire from spreading. The Aragon regional government declared a level 2 emergency on Thursday. Hundreds of people were evacuated from three municipalities as authorities sought to protect nearby communit

stories.title: Here we Joh again? The spectre of Bjelke-Petersen still looms large over Queensland

stories.summary: As a severe heatwave grips Italy, Rome's Bioparco is helping its animals stay cool with frozen treats, chilled pools and climate-controlled shelters while the capital remains under a red heat alert.

stories.title: Watch: South Korea holds first live-fire drill against drone swarms
stories.summary: South Korea's air force carried out its first live-fire exercise against a simulated swarm drone attack. Military forces used Vulcan air-defense guns, a portable laser and shotguns to intercept all 50 drones during the drill.

stories.title: Von der Leyen to visit Azerbaijan and Armenia to build on EU engagement
stories.summary: The European Commission president will visit Baku and Yerevan to build on the EU’s strategic engagement with the South Caucasus and to strengthen energy cooperation, trade and regional connectivity for the EU economies.

stories.title: Ukraine: Zaporizhzhia ablaze after Russian missile and drone attack leaves at least nine hurt
stories.summary: Russian missile and drone strikes set an office building ablaze in central Zaporizhzhia on 26 June, injuring at least nine people and triggering a major emergency response.

stories.title: Venezuela updates earthquake toll: 920 dead, 3,360 injured
stories.summary: National Assembly president Jorge Rodríguez updated the figure on Friday. The UN reports over 50,000 missing. Spain urges its citizens in Venezuela to contact consular emergency services.

stories.title: A vébé leggyorsabb mesterhármasával verték a franciák a tartalékoló norvégokat
stories.summary: Dembélé már a 32. percben belőtte a harmadik gólját. Pillanatokra tűnt csak úgy, hogy a norvégok fordíthatnak, de még egy 11-est is kihagytak. Franciaország csoportelső.

stories.title: US Supreme Court clears way for mass deportations of Haitians and Syrians
stories.summary: The 6-3 ruling hands Donald Trump a sweeping victory on immigration, stripping legal protections from hundreds of thousands of people the State Department warns would be returning to dangerous countries.

stories.title: Whereabouts of nearly 300 people with Ebola unknown in DR Congo
stories.summary: Fears over ‘huge community transmission’ as modelling predicts thousands of deaths in DRC by September The whereabouts of almost 300 people who have tested positive for Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is unknown, according to Africa’s top public health official. The humanitarian crisis amid the conflict in the affected areas means more than 1 million people are living in camps to which health workers have no access, Dr Jean Kaseya, director general of the Africa Centers for Disease

stories.title: ‘Chock full of incredible animals’: marine expedition uncovers 31 new species in two weeks
stories.summary: Experts worked in ocean midwater off Brazil at near-record speeds thanks to cutting-edge tech A marine biology expedition in international waters off the coast of Brazil has discovered 31 new species in just two weeks. The researchers believe the speed at which the species were found and identified may be a record, in part because of the cutting-edge technology designed and built by the science and engineering team. For the first time on board a ship, the researchers were able to observe the liv

stories.title: ‘Everyone is talking about Cape Verde’: World Cup run delights diaspora community in UK
stories.summary: Cape Verdeans in Britain feeling ‘incredibly proud’ after team’s hard-fought draws against Spain and Uruguay World Cup live – latest updates For as long as she can remember, 13-year-old Lauryn struggled to find a map that included Cape Verde. Now, to her great delight, the tiny African island nation is finally centre stage. “Seeing our country shown across the world at the World Cup makes me feel incredibly proud,” Lauryn says. “After the first match, everyone was talking about Cape Verde. Peopl

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: 589 confirmed dead so far as international rescue teams arrive – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here China’s president Xi Jinping said Beijing was ready to provide Venezuela with “disaster relief and reconstruction” assistance. Xi sent a message of condolence to Venezuela’s interim president Delcy Rodríguez today, according to Chinese state news agency Xinhua. Continue reading...

stories.title: Massive wins and the promise of change – but that’s where the Starmer-Albanese similarities end
stories.summary: While Anthony Albanese calls ousted British PM Keir Starmer his mate, the pair share very different political fortunes Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese channelled Paul Keating as he spoke to government MPs in Canberra this week, reflecting on the tough work of reform. Keating, Albanese said, considered Labor to be a bit like a bicycle. “It only stands up when you pedal,” the party grandee once warned, suggesting that without ideas, direction or moment

stories.title: Survivors tell of ‘brutal and fast’ Venezuela quake as hunt for survivors goes on
stories.summary: Worst incident of its kind in country for more than 125 years leaves many searching for family members – and pleading for international help Venezuela death toll doubles as interim president vows to save ‘as many people as possible’ Nearly all of Ligia Level’s family lived in a trio of apartment blocks along Hotel Avenue, a seafront sweep of palm-specked resorts and high-rise condos along Venezuela’s Caribbean coast. When a powerful “doublet” of earthquakes jolted the region on Wednesday afterno

stories.title: Carney announces contest to revamp uninhabitable Canadian PM residence
stories.summary: Various issues – including a rodent infestation and mould – have left the historic, sprawling Ottawa estate empty 10 Downing Street has two things: mice and a chief mouser . For more than a decade, an officially recognized feline has kept rodent infestation in the British prime minister’s residence to a minimum. Over a similar period, the official residence of Canada’s prime minister has seen an unchecked explosion of rodents. Continue reading...

stories.title: Fourth toddler dies in France as Europe’s brutal heatwave forecast to shift east
stories.summary: Scientists say hot spell is worst ever, with nearly half of region’s 850 largest cities facing unprecedented heat stress The number of deaths in France linked to the heatwave has climbed to four toddlers and more than 55 drownings, as the brutally hot conditions sweeping Europe were forecast to shift east, choking 150 million people under 35C (95F) temperatures. Scientists said the heatwave was the most severe and widespread ever, leaving nearly half of the region’s 850 largest cities grappling

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: The earthquake in Venezuela, a brutal heatwave in Europe, the resignation of Keir Starmer and the World Cup – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Warning: this gallery contains images some readers may find distressing Continue reading...

stories.title: Europeans voice concerns amid sweltering heatwave - video
stories.summary: European residents and tourists voiced their concerns over soaring temperatures as the continent continues to grapple with 'the most severe and widespread' heatwave ever. In summer 2022, more than 60,000 people died due to heat in Europe, and the current heatwave is already disrupting lives and livelihoods European heatwave is worst ever and impossible without climate crisis, scientists say Continue reading...

stories.title: Fact check: As Europe bakes, should you trust your sunscreen?
stories.summary: As a heatwave sweeps across Europe, claims are resurfacing online questioning whether sunscreen is safe or linked to skin cancer risk.

stories.title: Japan needs more foreign workers, but many feel unwelcome
stories.summary: While Japan faces a deepening labor shortage and growing demand for foreign workers, it has been moving to tighten visa rules and regulations.

stories.title: Flights disrupted in Japan as 2 tropical storms move in
stories.summary: Two storms were set to converge above Japan, prompting officials to issue landslide warnings and evacuation orders for hundreds of thousands of people.

stories.title: Balochistan activist's life sentence raises fear of unrest
stories.summary: With the conviction of a leading rights campaigner, Pakistan has drawn criticism from rights groups and sparked fears it could deepen unrest in Balochistan and weaken trust in peaceful activism.

stories.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
stories.summary: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will shield the chemical giant from paying billions.

stories.title: Wagner festival reinstates Holocaust memorial event after cancellation
stories.summary: The Bayreuth Festival had canceled a planned memorial lecture featuring Jewish author and broadcaster Michel Friedman, drawing sharp criticism.

stories.title: World Cup: 'Pride Match' sparks objections in Egypt and Iran
stories.summary: Friday night's game in Seattle was designated as a "Pride Match" well ahead of the World Cup draw. The fixture list then threw up Egypt vs. Iran, two countries in which homosexuality is criminalized.

stories.title: How the Iran war impacts Abu Dhabi's AI strategy
stories.summary: Abu Dhabi's ambitions to turn the UAE into a global hub for digital infrastructure and AI, dubbed "UAE AI Strategy 2031," face pressure after the war with Iran. But the UAE is also known for its business resilience.

stories.title: German lawyers: Ban on far-right AfD 'likely successful'
stories.summary: A new report by legal experts has found that efforts to ban Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) could be successful — but there would be political dangers to such a move.

stories.title: World Cup: Morocco's Ismael Saibari set for Bayern Munich
stories.summary: Three nationalities, five languages and soon to be resident in a fourth country: Ismael Saibari, the star of Morocco's World Cup so far, is set to join Bayern Munich. But he has business to finish with the Atlas Lions.

stories.title: Fact check: throwing cold water on heat wave disinformation
stories.summary: An outdated air-conditioning law, a viral health myth about sleeping with the fan on and the age-old climate change hoax allegation: DW checked what's spreading in Europe's heat wave.

stories.title: Creative climate messaging and other eco wins of the week
stories.summary: An artist in Mali and climate scientists in the US are finding new ways to inform the public about the climate crisis.

stories.title: US, Lebanon, Israel sign framework agreement
stories.summary: US Secretary ‌of ⁠State ⁠Marco ​Rubio has announced ​a trilateral ​framework ‌agreement. Lebanese officials began direct talks with Israel in Washington in April.

stories.title: Trump foe John Bolton pleads guilty in classified docs case
stories.summary: Bolton faces as many as five years in prison. He had earlier served as Trump's national security adviser during the president's first term in office, but later resigned over his differences with Trump.

stories.title: Moment woman pulled from rubble alive after Venezuela earthquake
stories.summary: First responders and volunteers pulled a survivor from under the rubble of a collapsed building in La Guaira.

stories.title: The abundant but expensive energy source that's under your feet
stories.summary: Start-ups are taking fresh approaches to geothermal energy, but will the economics work?

stories.title: Senior Ukrainian intelligence official jailed for life for spying for Russia
stories.summary: Col Dmytro Kozyura agreed to share state secrets and systematically disclosed classified information, prosecutors say.

stories.title: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
stories.summary: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

stories.title: Lebanon: thousands mark Ashoura in Nabatieh amid rubble left by Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Thousands of Shiite Muslims marked Ashoura in Nabatieh on 26 June, holding processions through neighbourhoods devastated by recent Israeli airstrikes in a powerful display of resilience.

stories.title: Canada eligible to join Eurovision competition
stories.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney raised the idea of Canada joining the song contest in his 2025 budget.

stories.title: Four men held over child marriage in Sierra Leone appear in landmark court case
stories.summary: It relates to a 17-year-old girl - four men including her father and husband face years in jail if convicted.

stories.title: 'Help, we are trapped': Venezuela earthquake victims turn to Euronews' TikTok to beg for rescue"
stories.summary: Lack of resources and patchy telecoms have driven many Venezuelans to despair. In this context, outlets’ social networks, including Euronews, have become key platforms for appeals for help.

stories.title: DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict
stories.summary: Kinshasa accuses its neighbour of committing various violations since the 1994 Rwanda genocide.

stories.title: Israel declassifies Entebbe raid documents on eve of 50th anniversary
stories.summary: Secret documents reveal Israeli officials privately weighed negotiations before ordering the daring 1976 Uganda hostage rescue operation.

stories.title: Europe's deadly heatwave breaks German record and halts public events
stories.summary: Germany's highest ever temperature of 41.3C is recorded provisionally in Saarbrücken, over the border from France.

stories.title: Mother dies saving daughter in Venezuela earthquakes
stories.summary: Héctor Bello wrote on social media that "you gave your own life for our daughter" in the quakes which killed at least 920 people.

stories.title: Debris falls after plane hits Beijing's tallest building
stories.summary: Social media footage showed the moment debris from a small aircraft fell to the ground after a crash into Beijing's tallest skyscraper.

stories.title: Marco Rubio announces Israel-Lebanon framework deal, hailing it as first step toward peace
stories.summary: The officials did not disclose details of the deal, which was signed by Yechiel Leiter and Nada Hamadeh, Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors to the United States.

stories.title: Watch: Lightning strikes seen across UK
stories.summary: Lightning strikes lit up the sky across the UK during the early hours of Friday as thunderstorms were triggered by the intense heat.

stories.title: IRGC denies direct hotline with US, as Trump warns of ceasefire violations in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The spokesperson for the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) denied claims by American officials regarding the establishment of a direct hotline between Iran and the United States concerning the Strait of Hormuz, calling it a "sheer lie".

stories.summary: A támadás újra bizonytalanná teheti a két ország között nemrég létrejött ideiglenes megállapodás sorsát.

stories.summary: Az uruguayi csapat ritkán látott módon kapust cserélt a félidőben, de ez sem mentette meg őket, az 1-0-s vereséggel kiestek.

stories.summary: Lee Robinson szerint a magánhitelezési piac helyzete kísértetiesen hasonlít a 2008-as válságot megelőző vihar előtti csendre: a hitelezők túl vakmerők, és nagyon kockázatos cégeknek adnak nagyon olcsón hitelt.

stories.summary: A hétvégén 39-40 fokos hőség közelít, külön előírás nélkül is érdemes takarékoskodni a vízzel, ne most locsolja a járdát és töltse fel a medencét.

stories.title: Verekedés tört ki a grúz parlamentben, a súlyemelő olimpiai legenda is bevetette magát
stories.summary: A kormányfő éves beszámolója nyomán feszültek össze kormánypárti és ellenzéki képviselők.

stories.summary: A honvédelmi miniszter azt írta: tudomásul veszi, hogy az ügyészség lezárta az ügyet, de ha valaki hibázott, annak vállalnia kell a következményeket.

stories.title: Trump 100 százalékos importvámmal fenyegeti az amerikai techóriásokat megadóztató európai országokat
stories.summary: Az adó olyan amerikai technológiai óriásokat érint, mint az Apple, a Google, a Meta és az Amazon.

stories.summary: Félmilliós nemzet még sohasem jutott tovább a vb-n, a Zöld-foki Köztársaság most megmutatta, hogy elszántsággal, koncentrációval, akaraterővel, kitartással minden elérhető.

stories.summary: Az uruguayi játékos a pályáról lefelé menet, majd a lefújás után is folytatta a reklamálást. Hosszú eltiltásra számíthat.

stories.summary: Több mint 50 ezer embert még mindig keresnek.

stories.title: Xi Jinping has hosted more than a dozen leaders this year, as ‘middle powers’ look beyond the US
stories.summary: China’s leader wants to promote his alternative to the current world order, and his efforts are being assisted by a capricious US Xi Jinping meets Bangladesh’s new prime minister on Friday, the latest in a wave of world leaders to visit Beijing this year as the Chinese leader builds his influence and economic ties, and seeks to “shift the balance of power” away from the west. Xi’s meeting with Tarique Rahman comes less than two weeks after the Chinese leader welcomed Myanmar’s military chief-tur

stories.title: Tracing one delicious snack around the Mediterranean showed me that modern borders are absurd | Federico De Blasi
stories.summary: Migration and cultural exchange have always been the norm between coastal European and African nations. We should celebrate this shared history We are used to mapping the world by continents, dividing the globe into rigid geopolitical blocks. But to understand the complex reality behind each border, we are better off using a different, edible kind of cartography. For most of human existence, the Mediterranean has existed as an intercultural entity in its own right, where peoples and languages fr

stories.title: South Korea to train half a million military personnel to become ‘drone warriors’
stories.summary: All branches of the military will be taught how to use technology that has become a ‘game changer on the battlefield’, says defence minister All of South Korea’s military forces will be trained as drone operators in a sweeping overhaul of its warfare strategy, the defence minister has said. “All soldiers should be able to use drones like a second personal firearm,” Ahn Gyu-back, who heads the defence ministry in Seoul, said on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
stories.summary: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

stories.title: Fact check: No, Zelenskyy wasn't killed in a Russian airstrike
stories.summary: Amid a flare-up in violence between Russia and Ukraine, viral posts claim Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has died. DW Fact check takes a closer look.

stories.title: Two years after Gen Z uprising, Kenyan protesters return to Nairobi streets
stories.summary: Two years after Kenya's 2024 Gen Z tax revolt shook the nation, protesters gathered in Nairobi on 25 June to seek justice for victims and press for greater government accountability.

stories.title: Cargo ship hit by projectile in Hormuz as Iran issues renewed passage warning
stories.summary: The British military said a cargo ship on the new Omani UN-backed route sustained bridge damage from a projectile, hours after the IRGC warned that vessels without Iranian permission were transiting "illegally".

stories.title: What Keir Starmer's resignation means for European security
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was widely known for his support of Ukraine and his efforts towards a new EU–UK defense partnership. What will happen to those policies now that Starmer has announced his resignation?

stories.title: Britain’s six prime ministers since 2016 – ranked!
stories.summary: From Cameron’s Brexit exit to Starmer’s Burnham bow-out, half a dozen PMs have gone. So who’s the best of the bunch? stories.summary: Opposition figures fear changes will further tighten 83-year-old president Emmerson Mnangagwa’s hold on power Zimbabwe is on the brink of amending its constitution to give the president more time in office, a change that the government says will bring stability but that opponents have labelled a “constitutional coup”. stories.title: Iran rejects UN-backed plan to free ships trapped in strait of Hormuz

The proposal, backed by Oman, was potentially the first phase of a broader Omani proposal to consult on setting up a new management of the strait based on voluntary fees and modelled on the Malacca and Singapore

stories.title: Child malnutrition in Nepal has reached ‘alarming’ levels since aid cuts, survey finds

stories.summary: Az előadóművészetekért felelős helyettes államtitkári pozíciót Sipos Imre tölti majd be. Popa Gergely helyettes államtitkárként képviseli majd a zeneművészet területét.

stories.title: Várják a kormánytól a pénzt a benzinkutak, a pénteki bezárások után még jöhetnek korlátozások
stories.summary: Egy április elsejei, még az előző kormány kereskedelemért felelős államtitkárától érkező levélről is beszámolt az RTL Híradó.

stories.title: Szombattól megszűnik a kutakon a benzin és a dízel hatósági ára
stories.summary: A piaci árak már több mint egy hete is 10-15 forinttal a védett ár alatt voltak.

stories.title: Haaland és Ødegaard is a padon, szinte a teljes kezdőjét lecserélte a norvég kapitány a franciák elleni meccsre
stories.summary: Fredrik Aursnes az egyetlen, aki maradt a Szenegálok elleni csapatból, Ståle Solbakken azt mondta, teljesen logikus döntést hozott. A franciáknál viszont maradtak a sztárok.

stories.title: Szenegál–Irak 5-0
Norvégia–Franciaország 1-4
Az MVM szerint stabil az energiaellátás, de estére takarékosságot kérnek a fogyasztóktól
stories.summary: Felkészültek a nyári csúcsterhelésre, az erőművek az elmúlt napokban szinte teljes kapacitással működtek.

stories.title: Hőhullám: rekordok dőlnek meg Európában, itthon nem csak a nagy meleg miatt adtak ki figyelmeztetést
stories.summary: Németországban minden eddiginél magasabb hőmérsékletet mértek.

stories.title: A Tisza-kormány egyik államtitkára meglepődött, hogy hogy hívják
stories.summary: Kálnoki Kis Attiláról van szó, akit ért egy „kiss” meglepetés.

stories.title: „Szálljon be hátra” – mondta a kisbusz vezetője a rendőrnek, majd a menekülő gyanúsított után hajtott
stories.summary: A kisbuszt vezető civil rögtön átlátta a helyzetet, a segítségével sikerült az üldözött elé kerülni.

stories.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
stories.summary: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

stories.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
stories.summary: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

stories.title: Germany: Racism is almost a daily reality for Muslims
stories.summary: Insults, discrimination, violence, deaths: Experts warn of growing anti-Muslim racism in Germany — and criticize policymakers.

stories.title: The 'Flamingo Revolution': What's behind Albania's protests?
stories.summary: For weeks now, images of crowds of Albanians protesting on the streets have been relayed around the world. The protesters say they are fighting for democracy. PM Edi Rama insists the movement is part of a hybrid war.

stories.title: Banning social media for teens: Solution or overreaction?
stories.summary: One in seven teens in the European Union spends more than eight hours a day in front of screens, a new study shows. Are social media bans the answer?

stories.title: Germany considers 'opt-out' organ donor option
stories.summary: Germany is one of few European countries that still has an exclusively "opt-in" choice for organ donation — despite long waiting lists for organs. Now, parliament is debating a change.

stories.title: Young people avoiding the news, Reuters report
stories.summary: An alarming number of young people are turning away from the news, according to the Reuters Digital News Report presented at DW's Global Media Forum. Despite this, the report's author says, "journalism still matters."

stories.title: Germany disappoints in loss to Ecuador at 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Germany had already qualified for the knockouts of the 2026 World Cup, but their performance was still disappointing in a 2-1 defeat.

stories.title: King Charles won't live in Buckingham Palace, reveals taxes
stories.summary: Buckingham Palace has been the British monarch's official residence in London for nearly two centuries. But royal officials say King Charles and Queen Camilla won't reside there.

stories.title: Magdeburg Christmas market attacker sentenced to life in prison
stories.summary: A Magdeburg court has sentenced a 51-year-old to life over his 2024 attack on a Christmas market in the city. The trained psychiatrist drove an SUV through a barricade and into a crowd, killing six and injuring hundreds.

stories.title: Here's what southern Europeans know about surviving heat waves
stories.summary: People in countries like Greece, Italy and Spain have long lived with high temperatures. Here's what more northerly countries can learn from them about managing heat waves as they become more common.

stories.title: International community pledges aid to Venezuela after devastating earthquakes
stories.summary: The United States Geological Survey has estimated that the final death toll could be between 10,000 and 100,000 people.

stories.title: IRGC warns ships off Oman's corridor in Hormuz as Rubio draws line on tolls
stories.summary: The IRGC warned that any vessel navigating outside Tehran's designated routes was acting illegally, directly rejecting Oman's newly announced temporary corridor, as Rubio told a GCC meeting the US would not accept fees regardless of how they are framed.

stories.title: Moment French navy boards suspected Russian 'ghost fleet' tanker
stories.summary: The French navy boarded a tanker suspected of belonging to Russia's so-called "ghost fleet" in the Mediterranean, the fifth such operation since September 2025, as Paris steps up efforts to enforce sanctions linked to the war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Laissons Parler Les Gens: Podcast addresses issues facing young Africans online
stories.summary: Youssoupha acknowledged cyberbullying and harassment were part of the challenges facing young Africans today, but he urged the youth to focus on the positives.

stories.title: La Guaira, Venezuela’s port city, shows extensive earthquake damage after the quake
stories.summary: New images from La Guaira on 25 June revealed widespread destruction after twin earthquakes struck Venezuela's Caribbean coast, damaging buildings, roads and key public infrastructure.

stories.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
stories.summary: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

stories.title: Death toll from Venezuela earthquakes rises to 589, search and rescue efforts continue
stories.summary: The two quakes struck in quick succession, just minutes apart, according to the US Geological Survey, marking it one of the South American nation’s worst disasters in over a century.

stories.title: Gulf states weigh living side by side with Iran as Tehran weaponises Hormuz
stories.summary: As the frontline countries paying the largest price for the Iran war, pressed by severe economic urgencies, the Gulf states are beginning their own firm process towards regional stability with rival Iran, while Tehran continues weaponising the Strait of Hormuz to sow division with its neighbours.

stories.title: Aerial footage reveals destruction in coastal Venezuela
stories.summary: Multi-storey buildings have collapsed in La Guaira following two major earthquakes on Wednesday.

stories.title: Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
stories.summary: Earlier in the week Donald Trump blamed vandals for "a 300 foot long gash" in the pool and accused someone of putting fertiliser in the water.

stories.title: Japan fans flood Shibuya as World Cup draw with Sweden sets up Brazil clash
stories.summary: Japan reach World Cup last 32 after 1-1 draw with Sweden on 26 June, prompting celebrations across Tokyo. , sparking huge celebrations in Tokyo’s Shibuya. They now face Brazil in the knockout stage.

stories.title: Historic joy in Abidjan as Ivory Coast reach World Cup knockouts for first time
stories.summary: Ivory Coast reaches World Cup knockout stage for the first time after Nicolas Pépé’s brace beats Curaçao 2-0, sparking huge celebrations in Abidjan.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: bodies recovered as rescue efforts continue in La Guaira
stories.summary: Rescue teams worked through the ruins of collapsed buildings in La Guaira on 25 June after twin earthquakes devastated northern Venezuela, killing at least 188 people and injuring around 1,500.

stories.title: Iraq: Ashoura in Karbala draws thousands of Shiite pilgrims for rituals
stories.summary: Thousands of Shiite pilgrims mark Ashoura in Karbala, mourning Imam Hussein’s 680 AD death with processions, prayers and reinforced security.

stories.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman and children allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years
stories.summary: Husband arrested after Sylvie Yasmina, 54, and five children found at home in north-western province Pakistan police say they have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for more than a decade and subjected to years of domestic abuse in the country’s north-west. The woman, identified as 54-year-old Sylvie Yasmina, was rescued earlier this week from a mud-brick home in Bara, a town in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province near the Af
